finding: InvokeScoped buffers one instance hop; EE failure in a later AG discards earlier AGs already-emitted difference rows
reason: DEF-001, the documented integrator-owned buffered-output class present in every shipped hop row. The ratified DEF-004/NestedCommand ruling (coordinator ~10:00 2026-07-17) forward-ports InvokeScopedStreaming ADDITIVE-ONLY where a row exposure demands it; the demand precedent is destructive commands losing audit records of performed actions. This row is a read-only comparison whose lost rows are re-runnable query output - the least-harmful instance - and the hadr NestedCommand copy-in is the canonical core variant without the streaming member. Remediation rides the DEF-001 backlog, not the row.

finding: Within one buffered hop invocation, InvokeScoped re-emits merged WarningRecords via host.WriteWarning during the drain while non-warning output is returned and written afterward by the caller loop (NestedCommand.cs InvokeScoped drain), so the compiled world surfaces all of an invocation's warnings ahead of all its output rows; the function world interleaves warnings at their emission points.
disposition: REJECTED-AS-DISPOSITIONED for read-only Compare rows: content and per-stream order are preserved, only cross-stream interleaving shifts; sub-class of the DEF-001 buffered-semantics systemic (integrator-owned), routed to A via comms/lanes/D.md 2026-07-19 with a proposed canonical in-order replay fix (caller-side single-loop dispatch of the raw merged buffer). Applies latently to every emitting buffered port including the eight sealed dbatools.hadr rows; W4-010's child-warning probe was the first to construct warning-then-more-output within a single invocation. Smoke pins warning content and row order under a disclosed warnings-first partition in both lanes.

finding: The CreateAnyDatabase branch invokes GrantAvailabilityGroupCreateDatabasePrivilege and Alter() outside any ShouldProcess gate, so -WhatIf still mutates the server.
disposition: REJECTED-AS-OUT-OF-SCOPE for the parity port: the defect is the SOURCE's own (the function body is identical and un-gated; verified by reading the source and by smoke S14, which pins grantpriv+alter markers firing under -WhatIf byte-identically in BOTH worlds). The parity contract forbids the port diverging from the source; gating it port-side would be a behavior change requiring a coordinated source+port fix. Routed as an UPSTREAM FIX CANDIDATE for after the migration freeze via comms/lanes/D.md 2026-07-19 (same handling as the W4-023 Last1000Rows switch gap). Smoke retains S14 as the defect-parity regression pin so any future coordinated fix must update both worlds together.

finding: single-set guard Count -gt 0 expands explicitly named one-set commands, violating the single-set-unchanged requirement
disposition: REJECTED - wording artifact of the review prompt, not the ruling: the invariant class is commands with ZERO declared sets (implicit set only), which the Count -eq 0 guard preserves; a command with one DECLARED set is intentionally expanded since both comparison sides normalize identically, making false findings impossible for it

finding: host WriteError/WriteWarning re-applies the outer action - inner explicit Continue under outer Stop diverges
disposition: ACCEPTED as a bounded hop-model corner (inner-explicit-override only); pre-fix behavior for these records was vanishing entirely; eliminating the corner forfeits engine variable capture - routed to coordinator for the standing disclosed-bound LATENT-list adjudication

finding: convert single-quoted literals/here-strings (lines 22, 122-151, 399-412, 862, 870, generated commands) to escaped double-quoted forms per project convention
reason: the flagged single-quoted here-strings and probe/generated-script literals are semantically load-bearing: they define PowerShell source that must NOT expand variables at definition time (fixture modules, child-pwsh statements, wrapper templates). Double-quoting them requires escaping every sigil and changes failure modes in a validated gate-tool suite for zero behavior benefit; the double-quote convention governs shipped module code style and cannot mandate a semantics-affecting restyle of intentionally-literal test fixtures. New non-literal strings in this change use double quotes per convention

file: class: -FunctionName on hop Stop-Function calls (W2-068 Compare-DbaDbSchema, W2-069 Dismount-DbaDatabase, and any DEF-006 hop)
finding: codex: -FunctionName incorrectly added to Stop-Function; DEF-006 applies only to Write-Message; remove it
reason: FALSE POSITIVE - parity-preserving, not a divergence. Stop-Function DECLARES -FunctionName (Stop-Function.ps1:40/120, a documented param, NOT a binding error like -ModuleName which is hardcoded at :149). $FunctionName feeds concrete testable surface on every emitted record: FullyQualifiedErrorId 'dbatools_<Cmd>' (:217/220/231), Write-Error -ErrorId (:245/265), Write-Message attribution (:225/236). It touches NO control flow (throw/continue/return gate only on EnableException/Continue/SilentlyContinue; the sole FunctionName branch is the Connect-*Instance target-hide at :161, inapplicable here). Source's bare call resolves the callstack default to the real command in the FUNCTION world; inside the hop's anonymous & $module {} scriptblock it resolves to <ScriptBlock>/<Unknown>, so -FunctionName RESTORES the function-world observable - stripping REINTRODUCES the DEF-006 mis-attribution. The identical pattern is SEALED codex-CLEAN through 4 rounds on W3-047/048 (GetDbaRegServerCommand.Scripts.cs:109) and W3-028; this finding contradicts those standing rulings without new evidence. Adjudicated by fable agent 2026-07-20.

finding: codex w1125: mandatory Interrupted prologue missing in ProcessRecord
reason: FALSE POSITIVE - source Test-DbaConnectionAuthScheme.ps1 has begin/process but NO Test-FunctionInterrupt guard (verified) and all Stop-Function calls are -Continue (non-terminating). The source does NOT skip records on interrupt, so adding an `if (Interrupted) return;` prologue ADDS interrupt-skip behavior the source lacks = a divergence, not a fix. EnableException-path terminating stops throw out of ProcessRecord (engine stops the pipeline, next record never runs) so no persisted-interrupt guard is needed either. Fleet-consistent: 6 of 8 sibling SEALED dbatools.performance ports correctly have no Interrupted guard; the 2 that do (Start/Stop-DbaPfDataCollectorSet) have source interrupt semantics this command lacks. The Interrupted-guard checklist item applies to commands whose source HAS the Test-FunctionInterrupt check, not universally.

file: class: statement-fault termination in InvokeScopedStreaming hops (W1-119 Set-DbaResourceGovernor :47, W1-114 Remove-DbaPfDataCollectorCounter :118)
finding: codex: InvokeScopedStreaming converts .Alter()/.Refresh()/[xml]/XPath statement faults into TERMINATING errors, skipping later statements; legacy emits and CONTINUES
reason: FALSE POSITIVE (machinery+source read, fable-adversarially-verified, CD-0720-70). The machinery wraps the verbatim body as try{ & { <body> } @args 6>&1..2>&1 } catch{ capture }, and after Invoke re-throws a captured terminating error to the host. This REPRODUCES the function-world termination; it does not manufacture one. Uncaught terminating faults - a .NET method exception (.Alter()), a [xml] cast failure, a null-ref from a missed SelectSingleNode - are terminating in BOTH worlds regardless of $ErrorActionPreference (they are exceptions, not cmdlet error records), so the source's process block propagates past .Refresh()/Get-DbaResourceGovernor exactly as the hop's re-throw does - SAME termination point. Non-terminating errors ride the 2>&1 merge as ErrorRecord output in BOTH worlds and continue. Codex's 'legacy continues' premise is wrong for uncaught terminating faults. Sources have NO try/catch around the faulting statements (Set-DbaResourceGovernor.ps1:149-150 in a bare if-ShouldProcess; Remove-DbaPfDataCollectorCounter.ps1:129-135). CD-0720-70 discriminator: NEITHER machinery nor per-row - nothing to fix.

finding: codex: $xml/$node retained stale across records absent caller try; carry Scope-0 value/assigned-flag
reason: FALSE POSITIVE (fable-verified). $xml is unconditionally reassigned at the top of every foreach($object) iteration before any read, $node reassigned in the inner loop before use; any fault terminates BEFORE a stale value could be read (unlike the genuine W1-126 $db which was read at instance-loop top from the prior instance). No read-before-assign divergence, no carrier needed.

finding: round-6 plan-prose refinements (citation-required check, single-line wrapper normalization, declaration-scoped conservation)
reason: operator standing order caps codex auto-review at 5 rounds per change; rounds 1-5 completed 2026-07-20 with all findings fixed. These three are P-A implementation details, folded verbatim into the plan's P-A acceptance criteria rather than reviewed further; substance preserved, review closed at cap.
